juries.name: Dr Amartya Mukhopadhyay
juries.research_details: Materials and Devices for Electrochemical Energy Storage · Electrode materials and interfaces for alkali metal-ion batteries (viz., Li-/Na-/K-ion); with special emphasis on the Na-ion chemistry · Solid-state and 'anodeless' Li/Na-based batteries · Electro-chemo-mechanical responses of electrode materials (including operando investigations) · Electrochemical energy storage devices; from concepts to materials to electrodes to cell to prototype

juries.name: Dr Udayan Ganguly
juries.research_details: Flash Memory materials and device optimization, Nanocrystal, nanotubes, nanowires growth and device characterization, Novel materials/process integration in CMOS platform, Chemical and bio sensors, Experimental device physics with modeling/design

juries.name: Dr Shalabh Gupta
juries.research_details: High-speed CMOS analog/RF/mm-wave integrated circuits and systems, Optical fiber communication systems, Microwave photonics / ultrafast data conversion using photonics, Beamforming antenna systems, Signal processing for these systems

juries.name: Dr Seshu S Pasumarthy
juries.research_details: Finite element modeling, Computational Solid Mechanics

juries.name: Dr Srinivas Seethamraju
juries.research_details: Computer aided design and simulation, Conceptual Design of processes , Fischer-Tropsch Synthesis, Thermodynamics of phase equilibria, Gasification , Reactive distillation, Integration of renewable energy resources with fossil energy

projects.title: Operando Analysis of Sodium-Ion Battery Electrode Interfaces
projects.description: This project investigates electrode materials and interfacial stability in sodium-ion batteries using operando electrochemical characterization techniques. The focus is on electro-chemo-mechanical responses during charge-discharge cycling and degradation analysis under high current densities. The study evaluates Na-ion chemistry, interface stability, and long-term performance of electrochemical energy storage devices. Prototype-level cell testing and materials-device integration are included to optimize energy density and cycling efficiency.

projects.title: Solid-State Anodeless Lithium-Based Battery Development
projects.description: The project focuses on the development of solid-state and anodeless Li-based batteries for next-generation electrochemical energy storage. Emphasis is placed on electrode materials, interface engineering, and prototype-level cell fabrication. The work includes modeling of electrochemical responses and performance validation under varying operating conditions.

projects.title: Modeling Electro-Chemo-Mechanical Degradation in Alkali Metal-Ion Cells
projects.description: This study models electro-chemo-mechanical coupling effects in alkali metal-ion battery electrodes. Special emphasis is given to Na-ion and Li-ion chemistry, interface stress evolution, and degradation pathways. The project integrates materials modeling with experimental validation to enhance long-term performance of electrochemical storage systems.

projects.title: Prototype Design of High-Energy-Density Na-Ion Electrochemical Storage Devices
projects.description: This project aims to design and prototype sodium-ion electrochemical energy storage devices with improved electrode material stability and interface durability. Solid-state battery architecture and cell-level integration are evaluated to enhance safety and energy density.

projects.title: High-Speed CMOS mm-Wave Integrated Circuit for Beamforming Antenna Systems
projects.description: This project develops a high-speed CMOS analog/RF integrated circuit operating in the mm-wave frequency range for beamforming antenna systems. The system integrates signal processing algorithms to enhance radiation pattern control and communication reliability. Performance evaluation includes bandwidth, gain, and power efficiency optimization.

projects.title: Microwave Photonics for Ultrafast Data Conversion in Optical Fiber Systems
projects.description: This project explores microwave photonics techniques for ultrafast data conversion in optical fiber communication systems. High-speed signal processing blocks are integrated with photonic modulation circuits to improve bandwidth and reduce signal distortion.

projects.title: RF/mm-Wave Signal Processing Architectures for Next-Generation Communication
projects.description: The project focuses on the development of high-frequency RF/mm-wave integrated circuits combined with advanced signal processing algorithms. The architecture supports beamforming antenna arrays and high-speed CMOS analog front-end systems.

projects.title: Reactive Distillation and Thermodynamic Modeling for Fischer-Tropsch Synthesis
projects.description: This project involves computer-aided design and simulation of reactive distillation systems for Fischer-Tropsch synthesis. The work includes thermodynamics of phase equilibria modeling and integration of renewable energy resources into fossil-based process systems. Gasification feedstock analysis and conceptual design optimization are incorporated.
